title: logical shift of signed values
body:
---


Author Name: **Thomas Watts**
Original Redmine Issue: 349 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2011-05-12
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

With the following code
```
reg [5:0] bob;  
reg signed [32:0] jim;
jim = (1'sb1 << bob);

```
when bob = 4 I would expect jim to equal 16, with Verilator 3.812 jim = -15.

Caveat - this code was not written with Verilator in mind, so I was using
/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */
to get it to compile, as soon as you extend the lhs of the shift to 33 bits as requested by the warning, then obviously the problem goes away.  So I can understand fixing it might be unimportant, however it was causing code that worked in another simulator to fail.
